name: Lombardy (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the field, evoking the plains and Alpine valleys of Lombardy

description: A green field charged with a stylised white Rosa Camuna (a four-lobed rosette derived from Val Camonica rock art) at the centre.
history: Adopted by Regional Law n. 85 of 12 June 1975, designed by Bruno Munari, Bob Noorda, Roberto Sambonet, and Pino Tovaglia. The emblem itself, the Rosa Camuna, is taken from Bronze Age petroglyphs in Val Camonica.

features.symbolism: A green field charged with a stylised white Rosa Camuna, an ancient pre-Roman rock-engraving motif from Val Camonica adopted as the regional emblem.

features.role: Rosa Camuna (a stylised four-petalled rosette adapted from prehistoric petroglyphs)
features.symbolism: The Rosa Camuna is one of the most common motifs in the prehistoric rock engravings of Val Camonica (a UNESCO World Heritage site); adopted in 1975 as the regional emblem of Lombardy, it ties the modern region to its most ancient inhabitants.
